props.title: The Folder Won: Why 2026's Most Interesting Tools Keep Choosing Files Over Databases

props.text: A technical deep-dive that frames MiroShark's file-based persistence (one folder per simulation, every analytics layer cached as its own JSON) inside the larger 2026 local-first revival — Ink & Switch, DuckDB-over-Parquet, the unwinding of the cloud-as-source-of-truth assumption. Argues the choice to keep the folder is what made simulation forking trivial (PR #17), embedding free (PR #34), and the nine-day analytics sprint (PRs #32/#33/#35) possible.
